Title: An update for python-pip is now available for openEuler-20.03-LTS-SP4

Notes.Text: An update for python-pip is now available for openEuler-20.03-LTS-SP4
Notes.Title: Summary

Notes.Text:
%changelog * Sat Jul 13 2024 yangyuan <yangyuan32@huawei.com> - 23.3.1-2 - Fix CVE-2023-45803 and CVE-2024-37891

Security Fix(es):

A flaw was found in pip, the package installer for Python. A remote attacker can exploit this vulnerability by tricking a victim into installing a malicious Python wheel. This wheel contains specially crafted entry-point names that use directory traversal or absolute paths. This allows pip to write generated script wrappers outside the intended installation directory, leading to arbitrary file overwrite. This can severely impact system integrity and availability, and in certain scenarios, may lead to arbitrary code execution.(CVE-2026-8643)
Notes.Title: Description

Notes.Text:
An update for python-pip is now available for openEuler-20.03-LTS-SP4.

openEuler Security has rated this update as having a security impact of medium. A Common Vunlnerability Scoring System(CVSS)base score,which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVElink(s) in the References section.
Notes.Title: Topic

Vulnerabilities.Note: A flaw was found in pip, the package installer for Python. A remote attacker can exploit this vulnerability by tricking a victim into installing a malicious Python wheel. This wheel contains specially crafted entry-point names that use directory traversal or absolute paths. This allows pip to write generated script wrappers outside the intended installation directory, leading to arbitrary file overwrite. This can severely impact system integrity and availability, and in certain scenarios, may lead to arbitrary code execution.
